{"content":"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
2: // SPDX-License-Identifier: BUSL-1.1
3: 
4: package terraform
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"context"
8: 	"fmt"
9: 	"log"
10: 	"sort"
11: 	"sync"
12: 
13: 	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
14: 
15: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
16: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
17: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/logging"
18: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
19: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/provisioners"
20: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
21: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
22: )
23: 
24: // InputMode defines what sort of input will be asked for when Input
25: // is called on Context.
26: type InputMode byte
27: 
28: const (
29: 	// InputModeProvider asks for provider variables
30: 	InputModeProvider InputMode = 1 << iota
31: 
32: 	// InputModeStd is the standard operating mode and asks for both variables
33: 	// and providers.
34: 	InputModeStd = InputModeProvider
35: )
36: 
37: // ContextOpts are the user-configurable options to create a context with
38: // NewContext.
39: type ContextOpts struct {
40: 	Meta         *ContextMeta
41: 	Hooks        []Hook
42: 	Parallelism  int
43: 	Providers    map[addrs.Provider]providers.Factory
44: 	Provisioners map[string]provisioners.Factory
45: 
46: 	// PreloadedProviderSchemas is an optional map of provider schemas that
47: 	// were already loaded from providers by the caller. This is intended
48: 	// to avoid redundant re-fetching of schemas when the caller has already
49: 	// loaded them for some other reason.
50: 	//
51: 	// The preloaded schemas do not need to be exhaustive. Terraform will
52: 	// use a preloaded schema if available, or will load a schema directly from
53: 	// a provider if no preloaded schema is available.
54: 	//
55: 	// The caller MUST ensure that the given schemas exactly match those that
56: 	// would be returned from a running provider of the given type or else the
57: 	// runtime behavior is likely to be erratic.
58: 	//
59: 	// Callers must not access (read or write) the given map once it has
60: 	// been passed to Terraform Core using this field.
61: 	PreloadedProviderSchemas map[addrs.Provider]providers.ProviderSchema
62: 
63: 	UIInput UIInput
64: }
65: 
66: // ContextMeta is metadata about the running context. This is information
67: // that this package or structure cannot determine on its own but exposes
68: // into Terraform in various ways. This must be provided by the Context
69: // initializer.
70: type ContextMeta struct {
71: 	Env string // Env is the state environment
72: 
73: 	// OriginalWorkingDir is the working directory where the Terraform CLI
74: 	// was run from, which may no longer actually be the current working
75: 	// directory if the user included the -chdir=... option.
76: 	//
77: 	// If this string is empty then the original working directory is the same
78: 	// as the current working directory.
79: 	//
80: 	// In most cases we should respect the user's override by ignoring this
81: 	// path and just using the current working directory, but this is here
82: 	// for some exceptional cases where the original working directory is
83: 	// needed.
84: 	OriginalWorkingDir string
85: }
86: 
87: // Context represents all the context that Terraform needs in order to
88: // perform operations on infrastructure. This structure is built using
89: // NewContext.
90: type Context struct {
91: 	// meta captures some misc. information about the working directory where
92: 	// we're taking these actions, and thus which should remain steady between
93: 	// operations.
94: 	meta *ContextMeta
95: 
96: 	plugins *contextPlugins
97: 
98: 	hooks     []Hook
99: 	sh        *stopHook
100: 	uiInput   UIInput
101: 	graphOpts *ContextGraphOpts
102: 
103: 	l                   sync.Mutex // Lock acquired during any task
104: 	parallelSem         Semaphore
105: 	providerInputConfig map[string]map[string]cty.Value
106: 	runCond             *sync.Cond
107: 	runContext          context.Context
108: 	runContextCancel    context.CancelFunc
109: }
110: 
111: // (additional methods on Context can be found in context_*.go files.)
112: 
113: // NewContext creates a new Context structure.
114: //
115: // Once a Context is created, the caller must not access or mutate any of
116: // the objects referenced (directly or indirectly) by the ContextOpts fields.
117: //
118: // If the returned diagnostics contains errors then the resulting context is
119: // invalid and must not be used.
120: func NewContext(opts *ContextOpts) (*Context, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
121: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
122: 
123: 	log.Printf("[TRACE] terraform.NewContext: starting")
124: 
125: 	// Copy all the hooks and add our stop hook. We don't append directly
126: 	// to the Config so that we're not modifying that in-place.
127: 	sh := new(stopHook)
128: 	hooks := make([]Hook, len(opts.Hooks)+1)
129: 	copy(hooks, opts.Hooks)
130: 	hooks[len(opts.Hooks)] = sh
131: 
132: 	// Determine parallelism, default to 10. We do this both to limit
133: 	// CPU pressure but also to have an extra guard against rate throttling
134: 	// from providers.
135: 	// We throw an error in case of negative parallelism
136: 	par := opts.Parallelism
137: 	if par < 0 {
138: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
139: 			tfdiags.Error,
140: 			"Invalid parallelism value",
141: 			fmt.Sprintf("The parallelism must be a positive value. Not %d.", par),
142: 		))
143: 		return nil, diags
144: 	}
145: 
146: 	if par == 0 {
147: 		par = 10
148: 	}
149: 
150: 	plugins := newContextPlugins(opts.Providers, opts.Provisioners, opts.PreloadedProviderSchemas)
151: 
152: 	log.Printf("[TRACE] terraform.NewContext: complete")
153: 
154: 	return &Context{
155: 		hooks:     hooks,
156: 		meta:      opts.Meta,
157: 		uiInput:   opts.UIInput,
158: 		graphOpts: &ContextGraphOpts{},
159: 
160: 		plugins: plugins,
161: 
162: 		parallelSem:         NewSemaphore(par),
163: 		providerInputConfig: make(map[string]map[string]cty.Value),
164: 		sh:                  sh,
165: 	}, diags
166: }
167: 
168: func (c *Context) SetGraphOpts(opts *ContextGraphOpts) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
169: 	c.graphOpts = opts
170: 	return nil
171: }
172: 
173: func (c *Context) Schemas(config *configs.Config, state *states.State) (*Schemas, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
174: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
175: 
176: 	ret, err := loadSchemas(config, state, c.plugins)
177: 	if err != nil {
178: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
179: 			tfdiags.Error,
180: 			"Failed to load plugin schemas",
181: 			fmt.Sprintf("Error while loading schemas for plugin components: %s.", err),
182: 		))
183: 		return nil, diags
184: 	}
185: 	return ret, diags
186: }
187: 
188: type ContextGraphOpts struct {
189: 	// If false, skip the graph structure validation.
190: 	SkipGraphValidation bool
191: 
192: 	// Legacy graphs only: won't prune the graph
193: 	Verbose bool
194: }
195: 
196: // Stop stops the running task.
197: //
198: // Stop will block until the task completes.
199: func (c *Context) Stop() {
200: 	log.Printf("[WARN] terraform: Stop called, initiating interrupt sequence")
201: 
202: 	c.l.Lock()
203: 	defer c.l.Unlock()
204: 
205: 	// If we're running, then stop
206: 	if c.runContextCancel != nil {
207: 		log.Printf("[WARN] terraform: run context exists, stopping")
208: 
209: 		// Tell the hook we want to stop
210: 		c.sh.Stop()
211: 
212: 		// Stop the context
213: 		c.runContextCancel()
214: 		c.runContextCancel = nil
215: 	}
216: 
217: 	// Notify all of the hooks that we're stopping, in case they want to try
218: 	// to flush in-memory state to disk before a subsequent hard kill.
219: 	for _, hook := range c.hooks {
220: 		hook.Stopping()
221: 	}
222: 
223: 	// Grab the condition var before we exit
224: 	if cond := c.runCond; cond != nil {
225: 		log.Printf("[INFO] terraform: waiting for graceful stop to complete")
226: 		cond.Wait()
227: 	}
228: 
229: 	log.Printf("[WARN] terraform: stop complete")
230: }
231: 
232: func (c *Context) acquireRun(phase string) func() {
233: 	// With the run lock held, grab the context lock to make changes
234: 	// to the run context.
235: 	c.l.Lock()
236: 	defer c.l.Unlock()
237: 
238: 	// Wait until we're no longer running
239: 	for c.runCond != nil {
240: 		c.runCond.Wait()
241: 	}
242: 
243: 	// Build our lock
244: 	c.runCond = sync.NewCond(&c.l)
245: 
246: 	// Create a new run context
247: 	c.runContext, c.runContextCancel = context.WithCancel(context.Background())
248: 
249: 	// Reset the stop hook so we're not stopped
250: 	c.sh.Reset()
251: 
252: 	return c.releaseRun
253: }
254: 
255: func (c *Context) releaseRun() {
256: 	// Grab the context lock so that we can make modifications to fields
257: 	c.l.Lock()
258: 	defer c.l.Unlock()
259: 
260: 	// End our run. We check if runContext is non-nil because it can be
261: 	// set to nil if it was cancelled via Stop()
262: 	if c.runContextCancel != nil {
263: 		c.runContextCancel()
264: 	}
265: 
266: 	// Unlock all waiting our condition
267: 	cond := c.runCond
268: 	c.runCond = nil
269: 	cond.Broadcast()
270: 
271: 	// Unset the context
272: 	c.runContext = nil
273: }
274: 
275: // watchStop immediately returns a `stop` and a `wait` chan after dispatching
276: // the watchStop goroutine. This will watch the runContext for cancellation and
277: // stop the providers accordingly.  When the watch is no longer needed, the
278: // `stop` chan should be closed before waiting on the `wait` chan.
279: // The `wait` chan is important, because without synchronizing with the end of
280: // the watchStop goroutine, the runContext may also be closed during the select
281: // incorrectly causing providers to be stopped. Even if the graph walk is done
282: // at that point, stopping a provider permanently cancels its StopContext which
283: // can cause later actions to fail.
284: func (c *Context) watchStop(walker *ContextGraphWalker) (chan struct{}, <-chan struct{}) {
285: 	stop := make(chan struct{})
286: 	wait := make(chan struct{})
287: 
288: 	// get the runContext cancellation channel now, because releaseRun will
289: 	// write to the runContext field.
290: 	done := c.runContext.Done()
291: 
292: 	go func() {
293: 		defer logging.PanicHandler()
294: 
295: 		defer close(wait)
296: 		// Wait for a stop or completion
297: 		select {
298: 		case <-done:
299: 			// done means the context was canceled, so we need to try and stop
300: 			// providers.
301: 		case <-stop:
302: 			// our own stop channel was closed.
303: 			return
304: 		}
305: 
306: 		// If we're here, we're stopped, trigger the call.
307: 		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context: requesting providers and provisioners to gracefully stop")
308: 
309: 		{
310: 			// Copy the providers so that a misbehaved blocking Stop doesn't
311: 			// completely hang Terraform.
312: 			walker.providerLock.Lock()
313: 			ps := make([]providers.Interface, 0, len(walker.providerCache))
314: 			for _, p := range walker.providerCache {
315: 				ps = append(ps, p)
316: 			}
317: 			defer walker.providerLock.Unlock()
318: 
319: 			for _, p := range ps {
320: 				// We ignore the error for now since there isn't any reasonable
321: 				// action to take if there is an error here, since the stop is still
322: 				// advisory: Terraform will exit once the graph node completes.
323: 				p.Stop()
324: 			}
325: 		}
326: 
327: 		{
328: 			// Call stop on all the provisioners
329: 			walker.provisionerLock.Lock()
330: 			ps := make([]provisioners.Interface, 0, len(walker.provisionerCache))
331: 			for _, p := range walker.provisionerCache {
332: 				ps = append(ps, p)
333: 			}
334: 			defer walker.provisionerLock.Unlock()
335: 
336: 			for _, p := range ps {
337: 				// We ignore the error for now since there isn't any reasonable
338: 				// action to take if there is an error here, since the stop is still
339: 				// advisory: Terraform will exit once the graph node completes.
340: 				p.Stop()
341: 			}
342: 		}
343: 	}()
344: 
345: 	return stop, wait
346: }
347: 
348: func (c *Context) checkStateDependencies(state *states.State) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
349: 	if state == nil {
350: 		// no state is fine, first time execution etc
351: 		return nil
352: 	}
353: 
354: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
355: 	for providerAddr := range state.ProviderRequirements() {
356: 		if !c.plugins.HasProvider(providerAddr) {
357: 			if c.plugins.HasPreloadedSchemaForProvider(providerAddr) {
358: 				// If the caller provided a preloaded schema for this provider
359: 				// then we'll take that as a hint that the caller is intending
360: 				// to handle some of these pre-validation tasks itself and
361: 				// so we'll just optimistically assume that the caller
362: 				// has arranged for this to work some other way, or will
363: 				// return its own version of this error before calling
364: 				// into here if not.
365: 				continue
366: 			}
367: 			if !providerAddr.IsBuiltIn() {
368: 				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
369: 					tfdiags.Error,
370: 					"Missing required provider",
371: 					fmt.Sprintf(
372: 						"This state requires provider %s, but that provider isn't available. You may be able to install it automatically by running:\n  terraform init",
373: 						providerAddr,
374: 					),
375: 				))
376: 			} else {
377: 				// Built-in providers can never be installed by "terraform init",
378: 				// so no point in confusing the user by suggesting that.
379: 				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
380: 					tfdiags.Error,
381: 					"Missing required provider",
382: 					fmt.Sprintf(
383: 						"This state requires built-in provider %s, but that provider isn't available in this Terraform version.",
384: 						providerAddr,
385: 					),
386: 				))
387: 			}
388: 		}
389: 	}
390: 	return diags
391: }
392: 
393: // checkConfigDependencies checks whether the recieving context is able to
394: // support the given configuration, returning error diagnostics if not.
395: //
396: // Currently this function checks whether the current Terraform CLI version
397: // matches the version requirements of all of the modules, and whether our
398: // plugin library contains all of the plugin names/addresses needed.
399: //
400: // This function does *not* check that external modules are installed (that's
401: // the responsibility of the configuration loader) and doesn't check that the
402: // plugins are of suitable versions to match any version constraints (which is
403: // the responsibility of the code which installed the plugins and then
404: // constructed the Providers/Provisioners maps passed in to NewContext).
405: //
406: // In most cases we should typically catch the problems this function detects
407: // before we reach this point, but this function can come into play in some
408: // unusual cases outside of the main workflow, and can avoid some
409: // potentially-more-confusing errors from later operations.
410: func (c *Context) checkConfigDependencies(config *configs.Config) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
411: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
412: 
413: 	// This checks the Terraform CLI version constraints specified in all of
414: 	// the modules.
415: 	diags = diags.Append(CheckCoreVersionRequirements(config))
416: 
417: 	// We only check that we have a factory for each required provider, and
418: 	// assume the caller already assured that any separately-installed
419: 	// plugins are of a suitable version, match expected checksums, etc.
420: 	providerReqs, hclDiags := config.ProviderRequirementsConfigOnly()
421: 	diags = diags.Append(hclDiags)
422: 	if hclDiags.HasErrors() {
423: 		return diags
424: 	}
425: 	for providerAddr := range providerReqs {
426: 		if !c.plugins.HasProvider(providerAddr) {
427: 			if c.plugins.HasPreloadedSchemaForProvider(providerAddr) {
428: 				// If the caller provided a preloaded schema for this provider
429: 				// then we'll take that as a hint that the caller is intending
430: 				// to handle some of these pre-validation tasks itself and
431: 				// so we'll just optimistically assume that the caller
432: 				// has arranged for this to work some other way, or will
433: 				// return its own version of this error before calling
434: 				// into here if not.
435: 				continue
436: 			}
437: 			if !providerAddr.IsBuiltIn() {
438: 				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
439: 					tfdiags.Error,
440: 					"Missing required provider",
441: 					fmt.Sprintf(
442: 						"This configuration requires provider %s, but that provider isn't available. You may be able to install it automatically by running:\n  terraform init",
443: 						providerAddr,
444: 					),
445: 				))
446: 			} else {
447: 				// Built-in providers can never be installed by "terraform init",
448: 				// so no point in confusing the user by suggesting that.
449: 				diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
450: 					tfdiags.Error,
451: 					"Missing required provider",
452: 					fmt.Sprintf(
453: 						"This configuration requires built-in provider %s, but that provider isn't available in this Terraform version.",
454: 						providerAddr,
455: 					),
456: 				))
457: 			}
458: 		}
459: 	}
460: 
461: 	// Our handling of provisioners is much less sophisticated than providers
462: 	// because they are in many ways a legacy system. We need to go hunting
463: 	// for them more directly in the configuration.
464: 	config.DeepEach(func(modCfg *configs.Config) {
465: 		if modCfg == nil || modCfg.Module == nil {
466: 			return // should not happen, but we'll be robust
467: 		}
468: 		for _, rc := range modCfg.Module.ManagedResources {
469: 			if rc.Managed == nil {
470: 				continue // should not happen, but we'll be robust
471: 			}
472: 			for _, pc := range rc.Managed.Provisioners {
473: 				if !c.plugins.HasProvisioner(pc.Type) {
474: 					// This is not a very high-quality error, because really
475: 					// the caller of terraform.NewContext should've already
476: 					// done equivalent checks when doing plugin discovery.
477: 					// This is just to make sure we return a predictable
478: 					// error in a central place, rather than failing somewhere
479: 					// later in the non-deterministically-ordered graph walk.
480: 					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
481: 						tfdiags.Error,
482: 						"Missing required provisioner plugin",
483: 						fmt.Sprintf(
484: 							"This configuration requires provisioner plugin %q, which isn't available. If you're intending to use an external provisioner plugin, you must install it manually into one of the plugin search directories before running Terraform.",
485: 							pc.Type,
486: 						),
487: 					))
488: 				}
489: 			}
490: 		}
491: 	})
492: 
493: 	// Because we were doing a lot of map iteration above, and we're only
494: 	// generating sourceless diagnostics anyway, our diagnostics will not be
495: 	// in a deterministic order. To ensure stable output when there are
496: 	// multiple errors to report, we'll sort these particular diagnostics
497: 	// so they are at least always consistent alone. This ordering is
498: 	// arbitrary and not a compatibility constraint.
499: 	sort.Slice(diags, func(i, j int) bool {
500: 		// Because these are sourcelss diagnostics and we know they are all
501: 		// errors, we know they'll only differ in their description fields.
502: 		descI := diags[i].Description()
503: 		descJ := diags[j].Description()
504: 		switch {
505: 		case descI.Summary != descJ.Summary:
506: 			return descI.Summary < descJ.Summary
507: 		default:
508: 			return descI.Detail < descJ.Detail
509: 		}
510: 	})
511: 
512: 	return diags
513: }

{"content":"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.

4: package terraform
5: 
6: import (
7: 	"bytes"
8: 	"fmt"
9: 	"log"
10: 	"sort"
11: 	"strings"
12: 	"time"
13: 
14: 	"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty"
15: 
16: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/addrs"
17: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/collections"
18: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/configs"
19: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/instances"
20: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang"
21: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/lang/globalref"
22: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/moduletest/mocking"
23: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/plans"
24: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/providers"
25: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/refactoring"
26: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/states"
27: 	"github.com/hashicorp/terraform/internal/tfdiags"
28: )
29: 
30: // PlanOpts are the various options that affect the details of how Terraform
31: // will build a plan.
32: type PlanOpts struct {
33: 	// Mode defines what variety of plan the caller wishes to create.
34: 	// Refer to the documentation of the plans.Mode type and its values
35: 	// for more information.
36: 	Mode plans.Mode
37: 
38: 	// SkipRefresh specifies to trust that the current values for managed
39: 	// resource instances in the prior state are accurate and to therefore
40: 	// disable the usual step of fetching updated values for each resource
41: 	// instance using its corresponding provider.
42: 	SkipRefresh bool
43: 
44: 	// PreDestroyRefresh indicated that this is being passed to a plan used to
45: 	// refresh the state immediately before a destroy plan.
46: 	// FIXME: This is a temporary fix to allow the pre-destroy refresh to
47: 	// succeed. The refreshing operation during destroy must be a special case,
48: 	// which can allow for missing instances in the state, and avoid blocking
49: 	// on failing condition tests. The destroy plan itself should be
50: 	// responsible for this special case of refreshing, and the separate
51: 	// pre-destroy plan removed entirely.
52: 	PreDestroyRefresh bool
53: 
54: 	// SetVariables are the raw values for root module variables as provided
55: 	// by the user who is requesting the run, prior to any normalization or
56: 	// substitution of defaults. See the documentation for the InputValue
57: 	// type for more information on how to correctly populate this.
58: 	SetVariables InputValues
59: 
60: 	// If Targets has a non-zero length then it activates targeted planning
61: 	// mode, where Terraform will take actions only for resource instances
62: 	// mentioned in this set and any other objects those resource instances
63: 	// depend on.
64: 	//
65: 	// Targeted planning mode is intended for exceptional use only,
66: 	// and so populating this field will cause Terraform to generate extra
67: 	// warnings as part of the planning result.
68: 	Targets []addrs.Targetable
69: 
70: 	// ActionTargets represents the actions that should be triggered by this
71: 	// execution. This is incompatible with the `Targets` attribute, only one
72: 	// can be set. Also, Mode must be plans.RefreshOnly when using
73: 	// ActionTargets.
74: 	//
75: 	// TEMP: For now, only support a single entry in this slice.
76: 	ActionTargets []addrs.Targetable
77: 
78: 	// ForceReplace is a set of resource instance addresses whose corresponding
79: 	// objects should be forced planned for replacement if the provider's
80: 	// plan would otherwise have been to either update the object in-place or
81: 	// to take no action on it at all.
82: 	//
83: 	// A typical use of this argument is to ask Terraform to replace an object
84: 	// which the user has determined is somehow degraded (via information from
85: 	// outside of Terraform), thereby hopefully replacing it with a
86: 	// fully-functional new object.
87: 	ForceReplace []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
88: 
89: 	// DeferralAllowed specifies that the plan is allowed to defer some actions,
90: 	// so that a subset of the plan can be applied even if parts of it can't yet
91: 	// be planned at all. Plans that contain deferred actions can't converge in
92: 	// a single run, and their configuration must be planned again after the
93: 	// dependencies of their deferred objects are in a usable state. Various
94: 	// events can cause deferrals, including unknown values in count and
95: 	// for_each arguments, and deferral notices from providers. If
96: 	// DeferralAllowed is false, the plan will error upon encountering an object
97: 	// that would be unplannable until after the apply.
98: 	DeferralAllowed bool
99: 
100: 	// ExternalReferences allows the external caller to pass in references to
101: 	// nodes that should not be pruned even if they are not referenced within
102: 	// the actual graph.
103: 	ExternalReferences []*addrs.Reference
104: 
105: 	// ExternalDependencyDeferred, when set, indicates that the caller
106: 	// considers this configuration to depend on some other configuration
107: 	// that had at least one deferred change, and therefore everything in
108: 	// this configuration must have its changes deferred too so that the
109: 	// overall dependency ordering would be correct.
110: 	ExternalDependencyDeferred bool
111: 
112: 	// Overrides provides a set of override objects that should be applied
113: 	// during this plan.
114: 	Overrides *mocking.Overrides
115: 
116: 	// GenerateConfigPath tells Terraform where to write any generated
117: 	// configuration for any ImportTargets that do not have configuration
118: 	// already.
119: 	//
120: 	// If empty, then no config will be generated.
121: 	GenerateConfigPath string
122: 
123: 	// ExternalProviders are clients for pre-configured providers that are
124: 	// treated as being passed into the root module from the caller. This
125: 	// is equivalent to writing a "providers" argument inside a "module"
126: 	// block in the Terraform language, but for the root module the caller
127: 	// is written in Go rather than the Terraform language.
128: 	//
129: 	// Terraform Core will NOT call ValidateProviderConfig or ConfigureProvider
130: 	// on any providers in this map; it's the caller's responsibility to
131: 	// configure these providers based on information outside the scope of
132: 	// the root module.
133: 	ExternalProviders map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
134: 
135: 	// ForcePlanTimestamp, if not nil, will force the "plantimestamp" function
136: 	// to return the given value instead of the time when the plan operation
137: 	// started.
138: 	//
139: 	// This is here only to allow producing fixed results for tests. Don't
140: 	// use it for main code.
141: 	ForcePlanTimestamp *time.Time
142: 
143: 	// Forget if set to true will cause the plan to forget all resources. This is
144: 	// only allowd in the context of a destroy plan.
145: 	Forget bool
146: 
147: 	// Query is a boolean that indicates whether the plan is being
148: 	// generated for a query operation.
149: 	Query bool
150: 
151: 	// OverridePreventDestroy will override any prevent_destroy attributes
152: 	// allowing Terraform to destroy resources even if the prevent_destroy
153: 	// attribute is set. This can only be set during a destroy plan, and should
154: 	// only be set during the test command.
155: 	OverridePreventDestroy bool
156: 
157: 	// AllowRootEphemeralOutputs overrides a specific check made within the
158: 	// output nodes that they cannot be ephemeral at within root modules. This
159: 	// should be set to true for plans executing from within either the stacks
160: 	// or test runtimes, where the root modules as Terraform sees them aren't
161: 	// the actual root modules.
162: 	AllowRootEphemeralOutputs bool
163: }
164: 
165: // Plan generates an execution plan by comparing the given configuration
166: // with the given previous run state.
167: //
168: // The given planning options allow control of various other details of the
169: // planning process that are not represented directly in the configuration.
170: // You can use terraform.DefaultPlanOpts to generate a normal plan with no
171: // special options.
172: //
173: // If the returned diagnostics contains no errors then the returned plan is
174: // applyable, although Terraform cannot guarantee that applying it will fully
175: // succeed. If the returned diagnostics contains errors but this method
176: // still returns a non-nil Plan then the plan describes the subset of actions
177: // planned so far, which is not safe to apply but could potentially be used
178: // by the UI layer to give extra context to support understanding of the
179: // returned error messages.
180: func (c *Context) Plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
181: 	plan, _, diags := c.PlanAndEval(config, prevRunState, opts)
182: 	return plan, diags
183: }
184: 
185: // PlanAndEval is like [Context.Plan] except that it additionally makes a
186: // best effort to return a [lang.Scope] which can evaluate expressions in the
187: // root module based on the content of the generated plan.
188: //
189: // The scope will be nil if the planning process doesn't complete successfully
190: // enough to produce a valid evaluation scope. If the returned plan is nil
191: // then the scope will always be nil, but it's also possible for the scope
192: // to be nil even when the plan isn't, if the plan is not complete enough for
193: // the evaluation scope to produce consistent results.
194: func (c *Context) PlanAndEval(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
195: 	defer c.acquireRun("plan")()
196: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
197: 
198: 	// Save the downstream functions from needing to deal with these broken situations.
199: 	// No real callers should rely on these, but we have a bunch of old and
200: 	// sloppy tests that don't always populate arguments properly.
201: 	if config == nil {
202: 		config = configs.NewEmptyConfig()
203: 	}
204: 	if prevRunState == nil {
205: 		prevRunState = states.NewState()
206: 	}
207: 	if opts == nil {
208: 		opts = &PlanOpts{
209: 			Mode: plans.NormalMode,
210: 		}
211: 	}
212: 
213: 	moreDiags := c.checkConfigDependencies(config)
214: 	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
215: 	moreDiags = c.checkStateDependencies(prevRunState)
216: 	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
217: 
218: 	// If required dependencies are not available then we'll bail early since
219: 	// otherwise we're likely to just see a bunch of other errors related to
220: 	// incompatibilities, which could be overwhelming for the user.
221: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
222: 		return nil, nil, diags
223: 	}
224: 
225: 	providerCfgDiags := checkExternalProviders(config, nil, prevRunState, opts.ExternalProviders)
226: 	diags = diags.Append(providerCfgDiags)
227: 	if providerCfgDiags.HasErrors() {
228: 		return nil, nil, diags
229: 	}
230: 
231: 	switch opts.Mode {
232: 	case plans.NormalMode, plans.DestroyMode:
233: 		// OK
234: 	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
235: 		if opts.SkipRefresh && len(opts.ActionTargets) == 0 {
236: 			// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should prevent this
237: 			// combination of options - although it is okay if we are invoking
238: 			// actions.
239: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
240: 				tfdiags.Error,
241: 				"Incompatible plan options",
242: 				"Cannot skip refreshing in refresh-only mode. This is a bug in Terraform.",
243: 			))
244: 			return nil, nil, diags
245: 		}
246: 	default:
247: 		// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should not try to
248: 		// create a context for a mode that Terraform Core doesn't support.
249: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
250: 			tfdiags.Error,
251: 			"Unsupported plan mode",
252: 			fmt.Sprintf("Terraform Core doesn't know how to handle plan mode %s. This is a bug in Terraform.", opts.Mode),
253: 		))
254: 		return nil, nil, diags
255: 	}
256: 	if len(opts.ForceReplace) > 0 && opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
257: 		// The other modes don't generate no-op or update actions that we might
258: 		// upgrade to be "replace", so doesn't make sense to combine those.
259: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
260: 			tfdiags.Error,
261: 			"Unsupported plan mode",
262: 			"Forcing resource instance replacement (with -replace=...) is allowed only in normal planning mode.",
263: 		))
264: 		return nil, nil, diags
265: 	}
266: 	if opts.Forget && opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
267: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
268: 			tfdiags.Error,
269: 			"Unsupported plan mode",
270: 			"Forgetting all resources is only allowed in the context of a destroy plan. This is a bug in Terraform, please report it.",
271: 		))
272: 		return nil, nil, diags
273: 	}
274: 
275: 	if len(opts.ActionTargets) > 0 {
276: 		if len(opts.Targets) != 0 {
277: 			// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should prevent this
278: 			// combination of options.
279: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
280: 				tfdiags.Error,
281: 				"Incompatible plan options",
282: 				"Cannot include both targets and action invocations. This is a bug in Terraform.",
283: 			))
284: 			return nil, nil, diags
285: 		}
286: 
287: 		if opts.Mode != plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
288: 			// The CLI layer (and other similar callers) should prevent this
289: 			// combination of options.
290: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
291: 				tfdiags.Error,
292: 				"Incompatible plan options",
293: 				"Must be in RefreshOnlyMode when invoking actions. This is a bug in Terraform.",
294: 			))
295: 			return nil, nil, diags
296: 		}
297: 	}
298: 
299: 	// By the time we get here, we should have values defined for all of
300: 	// the root module variables, even if some of them are "unknown". It's the
301: 	// caller's responsibility to have already handled the decoding of these
302: 	// from the various ways the CLI allows them to be set and to produce
303: 	// user-friendly error messages if they are not all present, and so
304: 	// the error message from checkInputVariables should never be seen and
305: 	// includes language asking the user to report a bug.
306: 	varDiags := checkInputVariables(config.Module.Variables, opts.SetVariables)
307: 	diags = diags.Append(varDiags)
308: 
309: 	if len(opts.Targets) > 0 {
310: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
311: 			tfdiags.Warning,
312: 			"Resource targeting is in effect",
313: 			`You are creating a plan with the -target option, which means that the result of this plan may not represent all of the changes requested by the current configuration.
314: 
315: The -target option is not for routine use, and is provided only for exceptional situations such as recovering from errors or mistakes, or when Terraform specifically suggests to use it as part of an error message.`,
316: 		))
317: 	}
318: 
319: 	if opts.Query {
320: 		var hasQuery bool
321: 		for c := range config.AllModules() {
322: 			if len(c.Module.ListResources) > 0 {
323: 				hasQuery = true
324: 				break
325: 			}
326: 		}
327: 
328: 		if !hasQuery {
329: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
330: 				tfdiags.Error,
331: 				"No resources to query",
332: 				`The configuration does not contain any resources that can be queried.`,
333: 			))
334: 			return nil, nil, diags
335: 		}
336: 	}
337: 
338: 	var plan *plans.Plan
339: 	var planDiags tfdiags.Diagnostics
340: 	var evalScope *lang.Scope
341: 	switch opts.Mode {
342: 	case plans.NormalMode:
343: 		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.plan(config, prevRunState, opts)
344: 	case plans.DestroyMode:
345: 		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.destroyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)
346: 	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
347: 		plan, evalScope, planDiags = c.refreshOnlyPlan(config, prevRunState, opts)
348: 	default:
349: 		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", opts.Mode))
350: 	}
351: 	diags = diags.Append(planDiags)
352: 	// NOTE: We're intentionally not returning early when diags.HasErrors
353: 	// here because we'll still populate other metadata below on a best-effort
354: 	// basis to try to give the UI some extra context to return alongside the
355: 	// error messages.
356: 
357: 	// convert the variables into the format expected for the plan
358: 	varVals := make(map[string]plans.DynamicValue, len(opts.SetVariables))
359: 	varMarks := make(map[string][]cty.PathValueMarks, len(opts.SetVariables))
360: 	applyTimeVariables := collections.NewSetCmp[string]()
361: 	for k, iv := range opts.SetVariables {
362: 		// If any input variables were declared as ephemeral and set to a
363: 		// non-null value then those variables must be provided again (possibly
364: 		// with _different_ non-null values) during the apply phase.
365: 		if vc, ok := config.Module.Variables[k]; ok && vc.Ephemeral {
366: 			// FIXME: We should actually do this based on the final value
367: 			// in the named values state, rather than the value as provided
368: 			// by the caller, so we can take into account the transforms
369: 			// done during variable evaluation. This is a plausible starting
370: 			// point for now, though.
371: 			if iv.Value != cty.NilVal && !iv.Value.IsNull() {
372: 				applyTimeVariables.Add(k)
373: 			}
374: 			continue
375: 		}
376: 
377: 		// Non-ephemeral variables must remain unchanged between plan and
378: 		// apply, so we'll record their actual values.
379: 		if iv.Value == cty.NilVal {
380: 			continue // We only record values that the caller actually set
381: 		}
382: 
383: 		// Root variable values arriving from the traditional CLI path are
384: 		// unmarked, as they are directly decoded from .tfvars, CLI arguments,
385: 		// or the environment. However, variable values arriving from other
386: 		// plans (via the coordination efforts of the stacks runtime) may have
387: 		// gathered marks during evaluation. We must separate the value from
388: 		// its marks here to maintain compatibility with plans.DynamicValue,
389: 		// which cannot represent marks.
390: 		value, pvm := iv.Value.UnmarkDeepWithPaths()
391: 
392: 		// We use cty.DynamicPseudoType here so that we'll save both the
393: 		// value _and_ its dynamic type in the plan, so we can recover
394: 		// exactly the same value later.
395: 		dv, err := plans.NewDynamicValue(value, cty.DynamicPseudoType)
396: 		if err != nil {
397: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
398: 				tfdiags.Error,
399: 				"Failed to prepare variable value for plan",
400: 				fmt.Sprintf("The value for variable %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", k, err),
401: 			))
402: 			continue
403: 		}
404: 		varVals[k] = dv
405: 		varMarks[k] = pvm
406: 	}
407: 
408: 	// insert the run-specific data from the context into the plan; variables,
409: 	// targets and provider SHAs.
410: 	if plan != nil {
411: 		plan.VariableValues = varVals
412: 		plan.ApplyTimeVariables = applyTimeVariables
413: 		if len(varMarks) > 0 {
414: 			plan.VariableMarks = varMarks
415: 		}
416: 
417: 		// Append all targets into the plans targets, note that opts.Targets
418: 		// and opts.ActionTargets should never both be populated.
419: 		plan.TargetAddrs = opts.Targets
420: 		plan.ActionTargetAddrs = opts.ActionTargets
421: 	} else if !diags.HasErrors() {
422: 		panic("nil plan but no errors")
423: 	}
424: 
425: 	if plan != nil {
426: 		relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, plan)
427: 		diags = diags.Append(rDiags)
428: 		plan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs
429: 	}
430: 
431: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
432: 		// We can't proceed further with an invalid plan, because an invalid
433: 		// plan isn't applyable by definition.
434: 		if plan != nil {
435: 			// We'll explicitly mark our plan as errored so that it can't
436: 			// be accidentally applied even though it's incomplete.
437: 			plan.Errored = true
438: 		}
439: 		return plan, evalScope, diags
440: 	}
441: 
442: 	diags = diags.Append(c.checkApplyGraph(plan, config, opts))
443: 
444: 	return plan, evalScope, diags
445: }
446: 
447: // checkApplyGraph builds the apply graph out of the current plan to
448: // check for any errors that may arise once the planned changes are added to
449: // the graph. This allows terraform to report errors (mostly cycles) during
450: // plan that would otherwise only crop up during apply
451: func (c *Context) checkApplyGraph(plan *plans.Plan, config *configs.Config, opts *PlanOpts) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
452: 	if plan.Changes.Empty() {
453: 		log.Println("[DEBUG] no planned changes, skipping apply graph check")
454: 		return nil
455: 	}
456: 	log.Println("[DEBUG] building apply graph to check for errors")
457: 
458: 	_, _, diags := c.applyGraph(plan, config, opts.ApplyOpts(), true)
459: 	return diags
460: }
461: 
462: var DefaultPlanOpts = &PlanOpts{
463: 	Mode: plans.NormalMode,
464: }
465: 
466: // SimplePlanOpts is a constructor to help with creating "simple" values of
467: // PlanOpts which only specify a mode and input variables.
468: //
469: // This helper function is primarily intended for use in straightforward
470: // tests that don't need any of the more "esoteric" planning options. For
471: // handling real user requests to run Terraform, it'd probably be better
472: // to construct a *PlanOpts value directly and provide a way for the user
473: // to set values for all of its fields.
474: //
475: // The "mode" and "setVariables" arguments become the values of the "Mode"
476: // and "SetVariables" fields in the result. Refer to the PlanOpts type
477: // documentation to learn about the meanings of those fields.
478: func SimplePlanOpts(mode plans.Mode, setVariables InputValues) *PlanOpts {
479: 	return &PlanOpts{
480: 		Mode:         mode,
481: 		SetVariables: setVariables,
482: 	}
483: }
484: 
485: func (c *Context) plan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
486: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
487: 	if opts.Mode != plans.NormalMode {
488: 		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.plan with %s", opts.Mode))
489: 	}
490: 
491: 	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
492: 	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
493: 
494: 	return plan, evalScope, diags
495: }
496: 
497: func (c *Context) refreshOnlyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
498: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
499: 
500: 	if opts.Mode != plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
501: 		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.refreshOnlyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
502: 	}
503: 
504: 	plan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, prevRunState, opts)
505: 	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
506: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
507: 		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
508: 		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
509: 		// to help understand the errors.
510: 		return plan, evalScope, diags
511: 	}
512: 
513: 	// If the graph builder and graph nodes correctly obeyed our directive
514: 	// to refresh only, the set of resource changes should always be empty.
515: 	// We'll safety-check that here so we can return a clear message about it,
516: 	// rather than probably just generating confusing output at the UI layer.
517: 	if len(plan.Changes.Resources) != 0 {
518: 		// Some extra context in the logs in case the user reports this message
519: 		// as a bug, as a starting point for debugging.
520: 		for _, rc := range plan.Changes.Resources {
521: 			if depKey := rc.DeposedKey; depKey == states.NotDeposed {
522: 				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr)
523: 			} else {
524: 				log.Printf("[DEBUG] Refresh-only plan includes %s change for %s deposed object %s", rc.Action, rc.Addr, depKey)
525: 			}
526: 		}
527: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
528: 			tfdiags.Error,
529: 			"Invalid refresh-only plan",
530: 			"Terraform generated planned resource changes in a refresh-only plan. This is a bug in Terraform.",
531: 		))
532: 	}
533: 
534: 	// We don't populate RelevantResources for a refresh-only plan, because
535: 	// they never have any planned actions and so no resource can ever be
536: 	// "relevant" per the intended meaning of that field.
537: 
538: 	return plan, evalScope, diags
539: }
540: 
541: func (c *Context) destroyPlan(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
542: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
543: 
544: 	if opts.Mode != plans.DestroyMode {
545: 		panic(fmt.Sprintf("called Context.destroyPlan with %s", opts.Mode))
546: 	}
547: 
548: 	priorState := prevRunState
549: 
550: 	// A destroy plan starts by running Refresh to read any pending data
551: 	// sources, and remove missing managed resources. This is required because
552: 	// a "destroy plan" is only creating delete changes, and is essentially a
553: 	// local operation.
554: 	//
555: 	// NOTE: if skipRefresh _is_ set then we'll rely on the destroy-plan walk
556: 	// below to upgrade the prevRunState and priorState both to the latest
557: 	// resource type schemas, so NodePlanDestroyableResourceInstance.Execute
558: 	// must coordinate with this by taking that action only when c.skipRefresh
559: 	// _is_ set. This coupling between the two is unfortunate but necessary
560: 	// to work within our current structure.
561: 	if !opts.SkipRefresh && !prevRunState.Empty() {
562: 		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: calling Context.plan to get the effect of refreshing the prior state")
563: 		refreshOpts := *opts
564: 		refreshOpts.Mode = plans.NormalMode
565: 		refreshOpts.PreDestroyRefresh = true
566: 		refreshOpts.OverridePreventDestroy = false
567: 
568: 		// FIXME: A normal plan is required here to refresh the state, because
569: 		// the state and configuration may not match during a destroy, and a
570: 		// normal refresh plan can fail with evaluation errors. In the future
571: 		// the destroy plan should take care of refreshing instances itself,
572: 		// where the special cases of evaluation and skipping condition checks
573: 		// can be done.
574: 		refreshPlan, _, refreshDiags := c.plan(config, prevRunState, &refreshOpts)
575: 		if refreshDiags.HasErrors() {
576: 			// NOTE: Normally we'd append diagnostics regardless of whether
577: 			// there are errors, just in case there are warnings we'd want to
578: 			// preserve, but we're intentionally _not_ doing that here because
579: 			// if the first plan succeeded then we'll be running another plan
580: 			// in DestroyMode below, and we don't want to double-up any
581: 			// warnings that both plan walks would generate.
582: 			// (This does mean we won't show any warnings that would've been
583: 			// unique to only this walk, but we're assuming here that if the
584: 			// warnings aren't also applicable to a destroy plan then we'd
585: 			// rather not show them here, because this non-destroy plan for
586: 			// refreshing is largely an implementation detail.)
587: 			diags = diags.Append(refreshDiags)
588: 			return nil, nil, diags
589: 		}
590: 
591: 		// We'll use the refreshed state -- which is the  "prior state" from
592: 		// the perspective of this "destroy plan" -- as the starting state
593: 		// for our destroy-plan walk, so it can take into account if we
594: 		// detected during refreshing that anything was already deleted outside
595: 		// of Terraform.
596: 		priorState = refreshPlan.PriorState.DeepCopy()
597: 
598: 		// The refresh plan may have upgraded state for some resources, make
599: 		// sure we store the new version.
600: 		prevRunState = refreshPlan.PrevRunState.DeepCopy()
601: 		log.Printf("[TRACE] Context.destroyPlan: now _really_ creating a destroy plan")
602: 	}
603: 
604: 	destroyPlan, evalScope, walkDiags := c.planWalk(config, priorState, opts)
605: 	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
606: 	if walkDiags.HasErrors() {
607: 		// Non-nil plan along with errors indicates a non-applyable partial
608: 		// plan that's only suitable to be shown to the user as extra context
609: 		// to help understand the errors.
610: 		return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
611: 	}
612: 
613: 	if !opts.SkipRefresh {
614: 		// If we didn't skip refreshing then we want the previous run state to
615: 		// be the one we originally fed into the c.refreshOnlyPlan call above,
616: 		// not the refreshed version we used for the destroy planWalk.
617: 		destroyPlan.PrevRunState = prevRunState
618: 	}
619: 
620: 	relevantAttrs, rDiags := c.relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config, destroyPlan)
621: 	diags = diags.Append(rDiags)
622: 
623: 	destroyPlan.RelevantAttributes = relevantAttrs
624: 	return destroyPlan, evalScope, diags
625: }
626: 
627: func (c *Context) prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State) ([]refactoring.MoveStatement, refactoring.MoveResults, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
628: 	explicitMoveStmts := refactoring.FindMoveStatements(config)
629: 	implicitMoveStmts := refactoring.ImpliedMoveStatements(config, prevRunState, explicitMoveStmts)
630: 	var moveStmts []refactoring.MoveStatement
631: 	if stmtsLen := len(explicitMoveStmts) + len(implicitMoveStmts); stmtsLen > 0 {
632: 		moveStmts = make([]refactoring.MoveStatement, 0, stmtsLen)
633: 		moveStmts = append(moveStmts, explicitMoveStmts...)
634: 		moveStmts = append(moveStmts, implicitMoveStmts...)
635: 	}
636: 	moveResults, diags := refactoring.ApplyMoves(moveStmts, prevRunState, c.plugins.ProviderFactories())
637: 	return moveStmts, moveResults, diags
638: }
639: 
640: func (c *Context) prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults refactoring.MoveResults, targets []addrs.Targetable) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
641: 	if len(targets) < 1 {
642: 		return nil // the following only matters when targeting
643: 	}
644: 
645: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
646: 
647: 	var excluded []addrs.AbsResourceInstance
648: 	for _, result := range moveResults.Changes.Values() {
649: 		fromMatchesTarget := false
650: 		toMatchesTarget := false
651: 		for _, targetAddr := range targets {
652: 			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.From) {
653: 				fromMatchesTarget = true
654: 			}
655: 			if targetAddr.TargetContains(result.To) {
656: 				toMatchesTarget = true
657: 			}
658: 		}
659: 		if !fromMatchesTarget {
660: 			excluded = append(excluded, result.From)
661: 		}
662: 		if !toMatchesTarget {
663: 			excluded = append(excluded, result.To)
664: 		}
665: 	}
666: 	if len(excluded) > 0 {
667: 		sort.Slice(excluded, func(i, j int) bool {
668: 			return excluded[i].Less(excluded[j])
669: 		})
670: 
671: 		var listBuf strings.Builder
672: 		var prevResourceAddr addrs.AbsResource
673: 		for _, instAddr := range excluded {
674: 			// Targeting generally ends up selecting whole resources rather
675: 			// than individual instances, because we don't factor in
676: 			// individual instances until DynamicExpand, so we're going to
677: 			// always show whole resource addresses here, excluding any
678: 			// instance keys. (This also neatly avoids dealing with the
679: 			// different quoting styles required for string instance keys
680: 			// on different shells, which is handy.)
681: 			//
682: 			// To avoid showing duplicates when we have multiple instances
683: 			// of the same resource, we'll remember the most recent
684: 			// resource we rendered in prevResource, which is sufficient
685: 			// because we sorted the list of instance addresses above, and
686: 			// our sort order always groups together instances of the same
687: 			// resource.
688: 			resourceAddr := instAddr.ContainingResource()
689: 			if resourceAddr.Equal(prevResourceAddr) {
690: 				continue
691: 			}
692: 			fmt.Fprintf(&listBuf, "\n  -target=%q", resourceAddr.String())
693: 			prevResourceAddr = resourceAddr
694: 		}
695: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
696: 			tfdiags.Error,
697: 			"Moved resource instances excluded by targeting",
698: 			fmt.Sprintf(
699: 				"Resource instances in your current state have moved to new addresses in the latest configuration. Terraform must include those resource instances while planning in order to ensure a correct result, but your -target=... options do not fully cover all of those resource instances.\n\nTo create a valid plan, either remove your -target=... options altogether or add the following additional target options:%s\n\nNote that adding these options may include further additional resource instances in your plan, in order to respect object dependencies.",
700: 				listBuf.String(),
701: 			),
702: 		))
703: 	}
704: 
705: 	return diags
706: }
707: 
708: func (c *Context) postPlanValidateMoves(config *configs.Config, stmts []refactoring.MoveStatement, allInsts instances.Set) tfdiags.Diagnostics {
709: 	return refactoring.ValidateMoves(stmts, config, allInsts)
710: }
711: 
712: // findImportTargets builds a list of import targets from any import blocks in
713: // config.
714: func (c *Context) findImportTargets(config *configs.Config) []*ImportTarget {
715: 	var importTargets []*ImportTarget
716: 	for _, ic := range config.Module.Import {
717: 		importTargets = append(importTargets, &ImportTarget{
718: 			Config: ic,
719: 		})
720: 	}
721: 	return importTargets
722: }
723: 
724: // findForgetTargets builds a list of resources and a list of modules to be
725: // forgotten, based on any removed blocks in config.
726: func (c *Context) findForgetTargets(config *configs.Config) (forgetResources []addrs.ConfigResource, forgetModules []addrs.Module, diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
727: 	removeStmts, diags := refactoring.FindRemoveStatements(config)
728: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
729: 		return nil, nil, diags
730: 	}
731: 	for _, rst := range removeStmts.Values() {
732: 		if rst.Destroy {
733: 			// no-op
734: 		} else {
735: 			if fr, ok := rst.From.(addrs.ConfigResource); ok {
736: 				forgetResources = append(forgetResources, fr)
737: 			} else if fm, ok := rst.From.(addrs.Module); ok {
738: 				forgetModules = append(forgetModules, fm)
739: 			} else {
740: 				panic("Invalid ConfigMoveable type in remove statement")
741: 			}
742: 		}
743: 	}
744: 	return forgetResources, forgetModules, diags
745: }
746: 
747: func (c *Context) planWalk(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*plans.Plan, *lang.Scope, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
748: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
749: 	log.Printf("[DEBUG] Building and walking plan graph for %s", opts.Mode)
750: 
751: 	prevRunState = prevRunState.DeepCopy() // don't modify the caller's object when we process the moves
752: 	moveStmts, moveResults, moveDiags := c.prePlanFindAndApplyMoves(config, prevRunState)
753: 	diags = diags.Append(moveDiags)
754: 	if moveDiags.HasErrors() {
755: 		return nil, nil, diags
756: 	}
757: 
758: 	// If resource targeting is in effect then it might conflict with the
759: 	// move result.
760: 	diags = diags.Append(c.prePlanVerifyTargetedMoves(moveResults, opts.Targets))
761: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
762: 		// We'll return early here, because if we have any moved resource
763: 		// instances excluded by targeting then planning is likely to encounter
764: 		// strange problems that may lead to confusing error messages.
765: 		return nil, nil, diags
766: 	}
767: 
768: 	graph, walkOp, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
769: 	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
770: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
771: 		return nil, nil, diags
772: 	}
773: 
774: 	timestamp := time.Now().UTC()
775: 	if opts.ForcePlanTimestamp != nil {
776: 		// Some tests use this to produce stable results to assert against.
777: 		timestamp = *opts.ForcePlanTimestamp
778: 	}
779: 
780: 	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
781: 	if opts != nil {
782: 		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
783: 	}
784: 
785: 	// If we get here then we should definitely have a non-nil "graph", which
786: 	// we can now walk.
787: 	changes := plans.NewChanges()
788: 
789: 	// Initialize the results table to validate provider function calls.
790: 	// Hold reference to this so we can store the table data in the plan file.
791: 	funcResults := lang.NewFunctionResultsTable(nil)
792: 
793: 	walker, walkDiags := c.walk(graph, walkOp, &graphWalkOpts{
794: 		Config:                     config,
795: 		InputState:                 prevRunState,
796: 		ExternalProviderConfigs:    externalProviderConfigs,
797: 		DeferralAllowed:            opts.DeferralAllowed,
798: 		ExternalDependencyDeferred: opts.ExternalDependencyDeferred,
799: 		Changes:                    changes,
800: 		MoveResults:                moveResults,
801: 		Overrides:                  opts.Overrides,
802: 		PlanTimeTimestamp:          timestamp,
803: 		FunctionResults:            funcResults,
804: 		Forget:                     opts.Forget,
805: 	})
806: 	diags = diags.Append(walker.NonFatalDiagnostics)
807: 	diags = diags.Append(walkDiags)
808: 
809: 	allInsts := walker.InstanceExpander.AllInstances()
810: 
811: 	moveValidateDiags := c.postPlanValidateMoves(config, moveStmts, allInsts)
812: 	if moveValidateDiags.HasErrors() {
813: 		// If any of the move statements are invalid then those errors take
814: 		// precedence over any other errors because an incomplete move graph
815: 		// is quite likely to be the _cause_ of various errors. This oddity
816: 		// comes from the fact that we need to apply the moves before we
817: 		// actually validate them, because validation depends on the result
818: 		// of first trying to plan.
819: 		return nil, nil, moveValidateDiags
820: 	}
821: 	diags = diags.Append(moveValidateDiags) // might just contain warnings
822: 
823: 	if moveResults.Blocked.Len() > 0 && !diags.HasErrors() {
824: 		// If we had blocked moves and we're not going to be returning errors
825: 		// then we'll report the blockers as a warning. We do this only in the
826: 		// absense of errors because invalid move statements might well be
827: 		// the root cause of the blockers, and so better to give an actionable
828: 		// error message than a less-actionable warning.
829: 		diags = diags.Append(blockedMovesWarningDiag(moveResults))
830: 	}
831: 
832: 	// If we reach this point with error diagnostics then "changes" is a
833: 	// representation of the subset of changes we were able to plan before
834: 	// we encountered errors, which we'll return as part of a non-nil plan
835: 	// so that e.g. the UI can show what was planned so far in case that extra
836: 	// context helps the user to understand the error messages we're returning.
837: 	prevRunState = walker.PrevRunState.Close()
838: 
839: 	// The refreshed state may have data resource objects which were deferred
840: 	// to apply and cannot be serialized.
841: 	walker.RefreshState.RemovePlannedResourceInstanceObjects()
842: 	priorState := walker.RefreshState.Close()
843: 
844: 	driftedResources, driftDiags := c.driftedResources(config, prevRunState, priorState, moveResults)
845: 	diags = diags.Append(driftDiags)
846: 
847: 	var forgottenResources []string
848: 	for _, rc := range changes.Resources {
849: 		if rc.Action == plans.Forget {
850: 			// TODO KEM display resource ids
851: 			forgottenResources = append(forgottenResources, fmt.Sprintf(" - %s", rc.Addr))
852: 		}
853: 	}
854: 	if len(forgottenResources) > 0 {
855: 		diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
856: 			tfdiags.Warning,
857: 			"Some objects will no longer be managed by Terraform",
858: 			fmt.Sprintf("If you apply this plan, Terraform will discard its tracking information for the following objects, but it will not delete them:\n%s\n\nAfter applying this plan, Terraform will no longer manage these objects. You will need to import them into Terraform to manage them again.", strings.Join(forgottenResources, "\n")),
859: 		))
860: 	}
861: 	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, prevRunState)
862: 	// We must finish building a plan object, and cannot return early here.
863: 	var changesSrc *plans.ChangesSrc
864: 	var err error
865: 	if !schemaDiags.HasErrors() {
866: 		changesSrc, err = changes.Encode(schemas)
867: 		if err != nil {
868: 			diags = diags.Append(err)
869: 		}
870: 	}
871: 
872: 	plan := &plans.Plan{
873: 		UIMode:             opts.Mode,
874: 		Changes:            changesSrc,
875: 		DriftedResources:   driftedResources,
876: 		PrevRunState:       prevRunState,
877: 		PriorState:         priorState,
878: 		ExternalReferences: opts.ExternalReferences,
879: 		Overrides:          opts.Overrides,
880: 		Checks:             states.NewCheckResults(walker.Checks),
881: 		Timestamp:          timestamp,
882: 		FunctionResults:    funcResults.GetHashes(),
883: 
884: 		// Other fields get populated by Context.Plan after we return
885: 	}
886: 
887: 	if !schemaDiags.HasErrors() {
888: 		deferredResources, deferredDiags := c.deferredResources(schemas, walker.Deferrals.GetDeferredChanges())
889: 		diags = diags.Append(deferredDiags)
890: 		plan.DeferredResources = deferredResources
891: 
892: 		deferredActionInvocations, deferredActionInvocationsDiags := c.deferredActionInvocations(schemas, walker.Deferrals.GetDeferredActionInvocations())
893: 		diags = diags.Append(deferredActionInvocationsDiags)
894: 		plan.DeferredActionInvocations = deferredActionInvocations
895: 	}
896: 
897: 	// Our final rulings on whether the plan is "complete" and "applyable".
898: 	// See the documentation for these plan fields to learn what exactly they
899: 	// are intended to mean.
900: 	if !diags.HasErrors() {
901: 		if len(opts.Targets) == 0 && !walker.Deferrals.HaveAnyDeferrals() {
902: 			// A plan without any targets or deferred actions should be
903: 			// complete if we didn't encounter errors while producing it.
904: 			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is complete")
905: 			plan.Complete = true
906: 		} else {
907: 			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is incomplete")
908: 		}
909: 		if opts.Mode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
910: 			// In refresh-only mode we explicitly don't expect to propose any
911: 			// actions, but the plan is applyable if the state was changed
912: 			// in an interesting way by the refresh step.
913: 			plan.Applyable = !plan.PriorState.ManagedResourcesEqual(plan.PrevRunState) ||
914: 				!plan.PriorState.RootOutputValuesEqual(plan.PrevRunState) ||
915: 				len(plan.Changes.ActionInvocations) > 0
916: 		} else {
917: 			// For other planning modes a plan is applyable if its "changes"
918: 			// are not considered empty (by whatever rules the plans package
919: 			// uses to decide that).
920: 			plan.Applyable = !plan.Changes.Empty()
921: 		}
922: 		if plan.Applyable {
923: 			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is applyable")
924: 		} else {
925: 			log.Println("[TRACE] Plan is not applyable")
926: 		}
927: 	} else {
928: 		log.Println("[WARN] Planning encountered errors, so plan is not applyable")
929: 	}
930: 
931: 	// The caller also gets access to an expression evaluation scope in the
932: 	// root module, in case it needs to extract other information using
933: 	// expressions, like in "terraform console" or the test harness.
934: 	evalScope := evalScopeFromGraphWalk(walker, addrs.RootModuleInstance)
935: 
936: 	return plan, evalScope, diags
937: }
938: 
939: func (c *Context) deferredResources(schemas *Schemas, deferrals []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChange) ([]*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
940: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
941: 	var deferredResources []*plans.DeferredResourceInstanceChangeSrc
942: 
943: 	for _, deferral := range deferrals {
944: 		schema := schemas.ResourceTypeConfig(
945: 			deferral.Change.ProviderAddr.Provider,
946: 			deferral.Change.Addr.Resource.Resource.Mode,
947: 			deferral.Change.Addr.Resource.Resource.Type)
948: 
949: 		deferralSrc, err := deferral.Encode(schema)
950: 		if err != nil {
951: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
952: 				tfdiags.Error,
953: 				"Failed to prepare deferred resource for plan",
954: 				fmt.Sprintf("The deferred resource %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", deferral.Change.Addr, err)))
955: 			continue
956: 		}
957: 
958: 		deferredResources = append(deferredResources, deferralSrc)
959: 	}
960: 	return deferredResources, diags
961: }
962: 
963: func (c *Context) deferredActionInvocations(schemas *Schemas, deferrals []*plans.DeferredActionInvocation) ([]*plans.DeferredActionInvocationSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
964: 	var deferredActionInvocations []*plans.DeferredActionInvocationSrc
965: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
966: 	for _, deferral := range deferrals {
967: 		schema := schemas.ActionTypeConfig(deferral.ActionInvocationInstance.ProviderAddr.Provider, deferral.ActionInvocationInstance.Addr.Action.Action.Type)
968: 
969: 		deferralSrc, err := deferral.Encode(&schema)
970: 		if err != nil {
971: 			diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
972: 				tfdiags.Error,
973: 				"Failed to prepare deferred action invocation for plan",
974: 				fmt.Sprintf("The deferred action invocation %q could not be serialized to store in the plan: %s.", deferral.ActionInvocationInstance.Addr, err)))
975: 			continue
976: 		}
977: 
978: 		deferredActionInvocations = append(deferredActionInvocations, deferralSrc)
979: 	}
980: 	return deferredActionInvocations, diags
981: }
982: 
983: func (c *Context) planGraph(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, opts *PlanOpts) (*Graph, walkOperation, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
984: 	var externalProviderConfigs map[addrs.RootProviderConfig]providers.Interface
985: 	if opts != nil {
986: 		externalProviderConfigs = opts.ExternalProviders
987: 	}
988: 
989: 	if opts != nil && opts.OverridePreventDestroy && opts.Mode == plans.RefreshOnlyMode {
990: 		panic("you can't set OverridePreventDestroy during refresh operations.")
991: 	}
992: 
993: 	switch mode := opts.Mode; mode {
994: 	case plans.NormalMode:
995: 		// In Normal mode we need to pay attention to import and removed blocks
996: 		// in config so their targets can be added to the graph.
997: 		forgetResources, forgetModules, diags := c.findForgetTargets(config)
998: 		if diags.HasErrors() {
999: 			return nil, walkPlan, diags
1000: 		}
1001: 		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
1002: 			Config:                    config,
1003: 			State:                     prevRunState,
1004: 			RootVariableValues:        opts.SetVariables,
1005: 			ExternalProviderConfigs:   externalProviderConfigs,
1006: 			Plugins:                   c.plugins,
1007: 			Targets:                   opts.Targets,
1008: 			ForceReplace:              opts.ForceReplace,
1009: 			skipRefresh:               opts.SkipRefresh,
1010: 			preDestroyRefresh:         opts.PreDestroyRefresh,
1011: 			Operation:                 walkPlan,
1012: 			ExternalReferences:        opts.ExternalReferences,
1013: 			Overrides:                 opts.Overrides,
1014: 			ImportTargets:             c.findImportTargets(config),
1015: 			forgetResources:           forgetResources,
1016: 			forgetModules:             forgetModules,
1017: 			GenerateConfigPath:        opts.GenerateConfigPath,
1018: 			SkipGraphValidation:       c.graphOpts.SkipGraphValidation,
1019: 			queryPlan:                 opts.Query,
1020: 			overridePreventDestroy:    opts.OverridePreventDestroy,
1021: 			AllowRootEphemeralOutputs: opts.AllowRootEphemeralOutputs,
1022: 		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
1023: 		return graph, walkPlan, diags
1024: 	case plans.RefreshOnlyMode:
1025: 		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
1026: 			Config:                    config,
1027: 			State:                     prevRunState,
1028: 			RootVariableValues:        opts.SetVariables,
1029: 			ExternalProviderConfigs:   externalProviderConfigs,
1030: 			Plugins:                   c.plugins,
1031: 			Targets:                   append(opts.Targets, opts.ActionTargets...),
1032: 			ActionTargets:             opts.ActionTargets,
1033: 			skipRefresh:               opts.SkipRefresh,
1034: 			skipPlanChanges:           true, // this activates "refresh only" mode.
1035: 			Operation:                 walkPlan,
1036: 			ExternalReferences:        opts.ExternalReferences,
1037: 			Overrides:                 opts.Overrides,
1038: 			SkipGraphValidation:       c.graphOpts.SkipGraphValidation,
1039: 			AllowRootEphemeralOutputs: opts.AllowRootEphemeralOutputs,
1040: 		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
1041: 		return graph, walkPlan, diags
1042: 	case plans.DestroyMode:
1043: 		graph, diags := (&PlanGraphBuilder{
1044: 			Config:                    config,
1045: 			State:                     prevRunState,
1046: 			RootVariableValues:        opts.SetVariables,
1047: 			ExternalProviderConfigs:   externalProviderConfigs,
1048: 			Plugins:                   c.plugins,
1049: 			Targets:                   opts.Targets,
1050: 			skipRefresh:               opts.SkipRefresh,
1051: 			Operation:                 walkPlanDestroy,
1052: 			Overrides:                 opts.Overrides,
1053: 			SkipGraphValidation:       c.graphOpts.SkipGraphValidation,
1054: 			overridePreventDestroy:    opts.OverridePreventDestroy,
1055: 			AllowRootEphemeralOutputs: opts.AllowRootEphemeralOutputs,
1056: 		}).Build(addrs.RootModuleInstance)
1057: 		return graph, walkPlanDestroy, diags
1058: 	default:
1059: 		// The above should cover all plans.Mode values
1060: 		panic(fmt.Sprintf("unsupported plan mode %s", mode))
1061: 	}
1062: }
1063: 
1064: // driftedResources is a best-effort attempt to compare the current and prior
1065: // state. If we cannot decode the prior state for some reason, this should only
1066: // return warnings to help the user correlate any missing resources in the
1067: // report. This is known to happen when targeting a subset of resources,
1068: // because the excluded instances will have been removed from the plan and
1069: // not upgraded.
1070: func (c *Context) driftedResources(config *configs.Config, oldState, newState *states.State, moves refactoring.MoveResults) ([]*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
1071: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
1072: 
1073: 	if newState.ManagedResourcesEqual(oldState) && moves.Changes.Len() == 0 {
1074: 		// Nothing to do, because we only detect and report drift for managed
1075: 		// resource instances.
1076: 		return nil, diags
1077: 	}
1078: 
1079: 	schemas, schemaDiags := c.Schemas(config, newState)
1080: 	diags = diags.Append(schemaDiags)
1081: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
1082: 		return nil, diags
1083: 	}
1084: 
1085: 	var drs []*plans.ResourceInstanceChangeSrc
1086: 
1087: 	for _, ms := range oldState.Modules {
1088: 		for _, rs := range ms.Resources {
1089: 			if rs.Addr.Resource.Mode != addrs.ManagedResourceMode {
1090: 				// Drift reporting is only for managed resources
1091: 				continue
1092: 			}
1093: 
1094: 			provider := rs.ProviderConfig.Provider
1095: 			for key, oldIS := range rs.Instances {
1096: 				if oldIS.Current == nil {
1097: 					// Not interested in instances that only have deposed objects
1098: 					continue
1099: 				}
1100: 				addr := rs.Addr.Instance(key)
1101: 
1102: 				// Previous run address defaults to the current address, but
1103: 				// can differ if the resource moved before refreshing
1104: 				prevRunAddr := addr
1105: 				if move, ok := moves.Changes.GetOk(addr); ok {
1106: 					prevRunAddr = move.From
1107: 				}
1108: 
1109: 				newIS := newState.ResourceInstance(addr)
1110: 
1111: 				schema := schemas.ResourceTypeConfig(
1112: 					provider,
1113: 					addr.Resource.Resource.Mode,
1114: 					addr.Resource.Resource.Type,
1115: 				)
1116: 				if schema.Body == nil {
1117: 					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
1118: 						tfdiags.Warning,
1119: 						"Missing resource schema from provider",
1120: 						fmt.Sprintf("No resource schema found for %s when decoding prior state", addr.Resource.Resource.Type),
1121: 					))
1122: 					continue
1123: 				}
1124: 
1125: 				oldObj, err := oldIS.Current.Decode(schema)
1126: 				if err != nil {
1127: 					diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
1128: 						tfdiags.Warning,
1129: 						"Failed to decode resource from state",
1130: 						fmt.Sprintf("Error decoding %q from prior state: %s", addr.String(), err),
1131: 					))
1132: 					continue
1133: 				}
1134: 
1135: 				var newObj *states.ResourceInstanceObject
1136: 				if newIS != nil && newIS.Current != nil {
1137: 					newObj, err = newIS.Current.Decode(schema)
1138: 					if err != nil {
1139: 						diags = diags.Append(tfdiags.Sourceless(
1140: 							tfdiags.Warning,
1141: 							"Failed to decode resource from state",
1142: 							fmt.Sprintf("Error decoding %q from prior state: %s", addr.String(), err),
1143: 						))
1144: 						continue
1145: 					}
1146: 				}
1147: 
1148: 				ty := schema.Body.ImpliedType()
1149: 				var oldVal, newVal cty.Value
1150: 				oldVal = oldObj.Value
1151: 				if newObj != nil {
1152: 					newVal = newObj.Value
1153: 				} else {
1154: 					newVal = cty.NullVal(ty)
1155: 				}
1156: 
1157: 				if oldVal.RawEquals(newVal) && addr.Equal(prevRunAddr) {
1158: 					// No drift if the two values are semantically equivalent
1159: 					// and no move has happened
1160: 					continue
1161: 				}
1162: 
1163: 				// We can detect three types of changes after refreshing state,
1164: 				// only two of which are easily understood as "drift":
1165: 				//
1166: 				// - Resources which were deleted outside of Terraform;
1167: 				// - Resources where the object value has changed outside of
1168: 				//   Terraform;
1169: 				// - Resources which have been moved without other changes.
1170: 				//
1171: 				// All of these are returned as drift, to allow refresh-only plans
1172: 				// to present a full set of changes which will be applied.
1173: 				var action plans.Action
1174: 				switch {
1175: 				case newVal.IsNull():
1176: 					action = plans.Delete
1177: 				case !oldVal.RawEquals(newVal):
1178: 					action = plans.Update
1179: 				default:
1180: 					action = plans.NoOp
1181: 				}
1182: 
1183: 				change := &plans.ResourceInstanceChange{
1184: 					Addr:         addr,
1185: 					PrevRunAddr:  prevRunAddr,
1186: 					ProviderAddr: rs.ProviderConfig,
1187: 					Change: plans.Change{
1188: 						Action: action,
1189: 						Before: oldVal,
1190: 						After:  newVal,
1191: 					},
1192: 				}
1193: 
1194: 				changeSrc, err := change.Encode(schema)
1195: 				if err != nil {
1196: 					diags = diags.Append(err)
1197: 					return nil, diags
1198: 				}
1199: 
1200: 				drs = append(drs, changeSrc)
1201: 			}
1202: 		}
1203: 	}
1204: 
1205: 	return drs, diags
1206: }
1207: 
1208: // PlanGraphForUI is a last vestage of graphs in the public interface of Context
1209: // (as opposed to graphs as an implementation detail) intended only for use
1210: // by the "terraform graph" command when asked to render a plan-time graph.
1211: //
1212: // The result of this is intended only for rendering to the user as a dot
1213: // graph, and so may change in future in order to make the result more useful
1214: // in that context, even if drifts away from the physical graph that Terraform
1215: // Core currently uses as an implementation detail of planning.
1216: func (c *Context) PlanGraphForUI(config *configs.Config, prevRunState *states.State, mode plans.Mode) (*Graph, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
1217: 	// For now though, this really is just the internal graph, confusing
1218: 	// implementation details and all.
1219: 
1220: 	var diags tfdiags.Diagnostics
1221: 
1222: 	opts := &PlanOpts{Mode: mode}
1223: 
1224: 	graph, _, moreDiags := c.planGraph(config, prevRunState, opts)
1225: 	diags = diags.Append(moreDiags)
1226: 	return graph, diags
1227: }
1228: 
1229: func blockedMovesWarningDiag(results refactoring.MoveResults) tfdiags.Diagnostic {
1230: 	if results.Blocked.Len() < 1 {
1231: 		// Caller should check first
1232: 		panic("request to render blocked moves warning without any blocked moves")
1233: 	}
1234: 
1235: 	var itemsBuf bytes.Buffer
1236: 	for _, blocked := range results.Blocked.Values() {
1237: 		fmt.Fprintf(&itemsBuf, "\n  - %s could not move to %s", blocked.Actual, blocked.Wanted)
1238: 	}
1239: 
1240: 	return tfdiags.Sourceless(
1241: 		tfdiags.Warning,
1242: 		"Unresolved resource instance address changes",
1243: 		fmt.Sprintf(
1244: 			"Terraform tried to adjust resource instance addresses in the prior state based on change information recorded in the configuration, but some adjustments did not succeed due to existing objects already at the intended addresses:%s\n\nTerraform has planned to destroy these objects. If Terraform's proposed changes aren't appropriate, you must first resolve the conflicts using the \"terraform state\" subcommands and then create a new plan.",
1245: 			itemsBuf.String(),
1246: 		),
1247: 	)
1248: }

1250: // referenceAnalyzer returns a globalref.Analyzer object to help with
1251: // global analysis of references within the configuration that's attached
1252: // to the receiving context.
1253: func (c *Context) referenceAnalyzer(config *configs.Config, state *states.State) (*globalref.Analyzer, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
1254: 	schemas, diags := c.Schemas(config, state)
1255: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
1256: 		return nil, diags
1257: 	}
1258: 	return globalref.NewAnalyzer(config, schemas.Providers), diags
1259: }
1260: 
1261: // relevantResourcesForPlan implements the heuristic we use to populate the
1262: // RelevantResources field of returned plans.
1263: func (c *Context) relevantResourceAttrsForPlan(config *configs.Config, plan *plans.Plan) ([]globalref.ResourceAttr, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {
1264: 	azr, diags := c.referenceAnalyzer(config, plan.PriorState)
1265: 	if diags.HasErrors() {
1266: 		return nil, diags
1267: 	}
1268: 
1269: 	var refs []globalref.Reference
1270: 	for _, change := range plan.Changes.Resources {
1271: 		if change.Action == plans.NoOp {
1272: 			continue
1273: 		}
1274: 
1275: 		moreRefs := azr.ReferencesFromResourceInstance(change.Addr)
1276: 		refs = append(refs, moreRefs...)
1277: 	}
1278: 
1279: 	for _, change := range plan.Changes.Outputs {
1280: 		if change.Action == plans.NoOp {
1281: 			continue
1282: 		}
1283: 
1284: 		moreRefs := azr.ReferencesFromOutputValue(change.Addr)
1285: 		refs = append(refs, moreRefs...)
1286: 	}
1287: 
1288: 	var contributors []globalref.ResourceAttr
1289: 
1290: 	for _, ref := range azr.ContributingResourceReferences(refs...) {
1291: 		if res, ok := ref.ResourceAttr(); ok {
1292: 			contributors = append(contributors, res)
1293: 		}
1294: 	}
1295: 
1296: 	return contributors, diags
1297: }

{"content":"1: // Copyright (c) HashiCorp, Inc.
